---
title: "Apatite Geochemistry as a Proxy for PCDs Fertility"
subtitle: "EAES 480 Final Project"
author: "Santiago Ramos Galarza"
institute: "University of Illinois Chicago"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: [default, metropolis, metropolis-fonts]
    lib_dir: libs
    nature:
      ratio: "16:9"
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}

options(htmltools.dir.version = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(broom)
library(infer)
library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(knitr)
library(xaringanthemer)

# For better figure resolution
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.retina = 3, dpi = 70, fig.width = 6, fig.asp = 0.618, out.width = "80%")


# Load and clean data silently for the presentation
df <- read_csv("/cloud/project/project-starter/data/apatite_data.csv")
chondrite <- c(La=0.237, Ce=0.612, Pr=0.0912, Nd=0.457, Sm=0.148, 
               Eu=0.0563, Gd=0.199, Tb=0.0361, Dy=0.246, Ho=0.0546, 
               Er=0.160, Tm=0.0247, Yb=0.161, Lu=0.0246)

df_clean <- df %>%
  mutate(
    La_N = La139_ppm_mean / chondrite["La"],
    Ce_N = Ce140_ppm_mean / chondrite["Ce"],
    Pr_N = Pr141_ppm_mean / chondrite["Pr"],
    Nd_N = Nd146_ppm_mean / chondrite["Nd"],
    Sm_N = Sm147_ppm_mean / chondrite["Sm"],
    Eu_N = Eu153_ppm_mean / chondrite["Eu"],
    Gd_N = Gd157_ppm_mean / chondrite["Gd"],
    Eu_anomaly = Eu_N / sqrt(Sm_N * Gd_N),
    Tb_N = Tb159_ppm_mean / chondrite["Tb"],
    Dy_N = Dy163_ppm_mean / chondrite["Dy"],
    Ho_N = Ho165_ppm_mean / chondrite["Ho"],
    Er_N = Er166_ppm_mean / chondrite["Er"],
    Tm_N = Tm169_ppm_mean / chondrite["Tm"],
    Yb_N = Yb172_ppm_mean / chondrite["Yb"],
    Lu_N = Lu175_ppm_mean / chondrite["Lu"]
  )
```

# Introduction
```{r castle, echo = FALSE, out.width = "100%", fig.align = "center", fig.cap = "Image credit: Photo by Enjoy Illinois."}
include_graphics("img/Intro_picture.png")
```
---

# Introduction
## Data
177 individual laser spot analyses (LASS-ICP-MS) on apatite grains.

## Europium Anomaly
This works as a proxy to quantify the oxidation state of the magma.
$$Eu/Eu^* = \frac{Eu_N}{\sqrt{Sm_N \times Gd_N}}$$
```{r data-vis, echo=FALSE}
summary_table <- df_clean %>%
  group_by(Type) %>%
  summarize(
    Mean_Eu_Anomaly = mean(Eu_anomaly, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Eu_Anomaly = sd(Eu_anomaly, na.rm = TRUE),
    Count = n()
  )
summary_table %>%
  knitr::kable(format = "html")
```

---

# Introduction

## Objective
Evaluate the predictive power of apatite trace element chemistry and develop a robust classification model capable of distinguishing between fertile (PCD) and barren magmatic systems.
.center[
```{r spider-plot, echo=F, out.width = "60%"}
spider_data <- df_clean %>%
  mutate(Sample_ID = row_number()) %>% 
  select(Sample_ID, Type, ends_with("_N")) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = ends_with("_N"), 
    names_to = "Element", 
    values_to = "Normalized_Value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Element = str_remove(Element, "_N"))
ree_order <- c("La", "Ce", "Pr", "Nd", "Sm", "Eu", "Gd", "Tb", "Dy", "Ho", "Er", "Tm", "Yb", "Lu")
spider_data$Element <- factor(spider_data$Element, levels = ree_order)
ggplot(spider_data, aes(x = Element, y = Normalized_Value, color = Type)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = Sample_ID), alpha = 0.15) +
  stat_summary(aes(group = Type), fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.5) +
  stat_summary(aes(group = Type), fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 2) +
  scale_y_log10() + 
  theme_linedraw() +
  labs(
    title = "Chondrite-Normalized REE Spider Diagram",
    y = "Sample / Chondrite",
    color = "Deposit Type"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("PCD" = "#4C72B0", "Barren" = "#C44E52"))
```
]
---

#Exploratory Geochemistry
A visual comparison of the calculated $Eu/Eu^*$ ratio shows a distinct shift, with Barren samples exhibiting much stronger negative anomalies.
.center[
```{r plot, echo=F, out.width = "60%"}
ggplot(df_clean, aes(x = Type, y = Eu_anomaly, fill = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.color = "red") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  labs(y = expression("Eu / Eu*"), x = "") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("PCD" = "#4C72B0", "Barren" = "#C44E52")) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
]
---

#Statistical Significance
##Randomization Test
**Null Hypothesis ($H_0$):** There is no difference in the mean $Eu/Eu^*$ between PCD and Barren apatite. (The variables are independent)


**Alternative Hypothesis ($H_A$):** There is a significant difference in the mean $Eu/Eu^*$ between the two groups.
```{r randomization, echo=T}
set.seed(12)
obs_diff <- df_clean %>%
  specify(Eu_anomaly ~ Type) %>% calculate(stat = "diff in means", order = c("PCD", "Barren"))

null_dist <- df_clean %>%
  specify(Eu_anomaly ~ Type) %>%
  hypothesize(null = "independence") %>%
  generate(reps = 1000, type = "permute") %>%
  calculate(stat = "diff in means", order = c("PCD", "Barren"))
```
---
.center[
```{r randomization-plot, echo=F}
visualize(null_dist) +
  shade_p_value(obs_stat = obs_diff, direction = "two-sided") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  labs(
    title = "Simulation-Based Null Distribution",
    subtitle = "Red line: difference in means",
    x = "Simulated Difference in Means (PCD - Barren)",
    y = "Count"
  )
```
```{r p-value, echo=F}
p_value <- null_dist %>%
  get_p_value(obs_stat = obs_diff, direction = "two-sided")

p_value
```
]

---
#Statistical Significance
## Bootstrap

While the randomization test tells us the difference is significant.
Calculating the 95% Bootstrap CI quantifies the magnitude of this difference.

$$(\bar{x}_{PCD} - \bar{x}_{Barren})$$

```{r bootstrap-ci, echo=T}
set.seed(12)

# Bootstrap distribution
boot_dist <- df_clean %>%
  specify(Eu_anomaly ~ Type) %>%
  generate(reps = 1000, type = "bootstrap") %>%
  calculate(stat = "diff in means", order = c("PCD", "Barren"))

# 95% CI
percentile_ci <- boot_dist %>%
  get_confidence_interval(level = 0.95, type = "percentile")
```

---
.center[
```{r bootstrap-ci-plot, echo=F}
visualize(boot_dist) +
  shade_confidence_interval(endpoints = percentile_ci) +
  theme_linedraw() +
  labs(
    title = "Bootstrap Distribution",
    subtitle = "Shaded area represents the 95% Confidence Interval",
    x = "Mean Eu Anomaly (PCD - Barren)",
    y = "Count"
  )
percentile_ci %>%
  knitr::kable(format = "html")
```
]
---
#Statistical Significance
##Linear Modeling
To quantify the variance explained by the deposit type
```{r linear-modeling, echo=T}
eu_model <- lm(Eu_anomaly ~ Type, data = df_clean)
model_results <- augment(eu_model)
rmse_calc <- model_results %>% 
  summarize(RMSE = sqrt(mean(.resid^2)))
```

```{r linear-modeling-table, echo=F}
glance(eu_model) %>% 
  select(r.squared, p.value) %>%
  bind_cols(rmse_calc) %>%
  knitr::kable(format = "html", digits = 4)
```
- The model coefficient confirmed a increase in the $Eu/Eu^*$ when transitioning from Barren to PCD-fertile systems.

- A p-value of < 0.001 provided strong evidence to reject the null hypothesis, confirming that the relationship between rock type and Europium depletion is not due to random chance.
---

#Statistical Significance
## Comprehensive Analysis of All REEs

Instead of analyzing each REE individually, I iterated through all normalized REEs. This pipeline applies the simple linear model, the randomization test (1000 permutations), and the bootstrap CI (1000 resamples) to every geochemical variable.

```{r analyze-all-ree-p1, warning=F, echo=TRUE}
# Group by the Element name
df_long <- df_clean %>%
  select(Type, La_N:Lu_N) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -Type, names_to = "Element", values_to = "Value") %>%
  glimpse()
```
---
#Statistical Significance
```{r analyze-all-ree-p2, warning=F, echo=TRUE}
analyze_element <- function(sub_df) {
  set.seed(12)
# Slope and R-Squared
  fit <- lm(Value ~ Type, data = sub_df)
  tidy_fit <- tidy(fit) %>% filter(term == "TypePCD")
  glance_fit <- glance(fit)
# Observed Difference
  obs <- sub_df %>%
    specify(Value ~ Type) %>%
    calculate(stat = "diff in means", order = c("PCD", "Barren"))
# Randomization Test
  null_dist <- sub_df %>%
    specify(Value ~ Type) %>%
    hypothesize(null = "independence") %>%
    generate(reps = 1000, type = "permute") %>%
    calculate(stat = "diff in means", order = c("PCD", "Barren"))
  p_val <- null_dist %>% get_p_value(obs_stat = obs, direction = "two-sided")
# Bootstrap CI
  boot_dist <- sub_df %>%
    specify(Value ~ Type) %>%
    generate(reps = 1000, type = "bootstrap") %>%
    calculate(stat = "diff in means", order = c("PCD", "Barren"))
  ci <- boot_dist %>% get_confidence_interval(level = 0.95, type = "percentile")
  
  # Summarize
  tibble(
    Difference_in_Means = tidy_fit$estimate,
    R_Squared = glance_fit$r.squared,
    Rand_P_Value = p_val$p_value,
    CI_Lower = ci$lower_ci,
    CI_Upper = ci$upper_ci
  )
}
```

---
#Statistical Significance
```{r table-all-ree, warning=F, echo=F}
# Summary table
all_results <- df_long %>%
  nest(.by = Element) %>% # Group data by Element into 'lists'
  mutate(results = map(data, analyze_element)) %>% # Run the custom function
  unnest(results) %>% 
  select(-data) %>%
  arrange(Rand_P_Value, desc(R_Squared)) 

knitr::kable(all_results, digits = 4)
```

---
#Classification Tree

Using all 14 REEs simultaneously to find the optimal geochemical thresholds.

```{r tree, echo=T}
tree_data <- df_clean %>%
  select(Type, Eu_anomaly, La_N:Lu_N)

tree_spec <- decision_tree() %>%
  set_mode("classification") %>%
  set_engine("rpart")

tree_recipe <- recipe(Type ~ ., data = tree_data)

tree_workflow <- workflow() %>%
  add_recipe(tree_recipe) %>%
  add_model(tree_spec)


tree_fit <- tree_workflow %>%
  fit(data = tree_data)
```


---
#Classification Tree
.center[
```{r warning=F, tree-plot, echo=F}
tree_engine <- extract_fit_engine(tree_fit)

rpart.plot(tree_engine, 
           main = "Classification Tree for Apatite Fertility",
           type = 3,           
           extra = 104,        
           box.palette = c("#C44E52", "#4C72B0"), 
           shadow.col = "gray",
           fallen.leaves = TRUE)
```
]

---
#Conclusions

<div style="font-size: 28px;">
- The Europium anomaly ($Eu/Eu^*$) is a statistically robust indicator of magmatic fertility.

--

- Classification Trees revealed that multi-element evaluation is superior. 

--

- LREE enrichment ($Pr_N$) identifies highly fractionated, barren magmatic systems before redox conditions are even considered.

--

- The oxidation state (represented by the $Eu$ anomaly) acts as the definitive secondary split for fertility.
